context_summary: Phase 19 marked complete (98a3f8c) with PASS-WITH-DEFICIT. Post-completion debug session uncovered: (1) bench wall_clock_ms is bug-contaminated by background-task sleep cycles for N<500k; (2) WAL has bimodal-tail (1% of events stall 1-42ms each); (3) large-with-sketches apply-bound at 250k EPS theoretical with sketches consuming 76% of features-time; (4) WindowedOp's preallocated 64-slot bucket array is the cold-key allocation hotspot. Three Phase 19.0.x hotfix candidates queued.

uncommitted_files.note: research output from background agent — 110 features, 15 categories, 14 sources
14-node pipeline config; 7 of 10 lifetime-only-with-window violations fixed; 3+ remaining param-shape errors (e.g. histogram bins vs buckets)
gsd-code-reviewer output from earlier in session

experimental_changes_reverted.what: Bumped WAL buffers from 3x16MiB tick=2ms to 8x64MiB tick=100ms
experimental_changes_reverted.result: Bimodal wal_append tail eliminated; throughput +33% on cache-hot fixed shape
experimental_changes_reverted.why_reverted: Needs proper Phase 19.0.1 plan with TDD; 8x64MiB = 512MB resident is too aggressive; 4x32MiB likely the right middle ground

decisions.decision: Per-op trace tool kept (committed) as production-safe debug aid
decisions.rationale: Validates first-principles latency analysis; future optimizations can confirm gains

decisions.decision: WAL config bump experimental — needs proper plan
decisions.rationale: 8x64MiB removes bimodal tail BUT 512MB RSS too heavy for default

decisions.decision: Phase 19 verdict needs re-baselining after wall_clock fix
decisions.rationale: PASS-WITH-DEFICIT was based on bug-contaminated number; real number clears M4 threshold

open_questions: Default WAL config: 4x32MiB tick=20ms middle ground, or just fix wall_clock?
Histogram windowed semantics: add windowed_histogram op family, or document UDDSketch as the path?
Fraud-team config: ship as bench config or as /beava skill template?
Phase 19.0.x numbering: open 3 small phases or one combined Phase 19.1?

wall_clock_bug.issue: elapsed = start.elapsed() captured AFTER awaiting get_task (1s sleep) and rss_task (500ms sleep)
wall_clock_bug.symptom: For N < ~500k, wall_clock dominated by background-task shutdown latency, not throughput
wall_clock_bug.impact: Phase 19 published canonical-cell number 5x under-reported (106k vs 536k)

wal_bimodal_tail.issue: 1% of events have wal_append > 1ms (range 1-42ms) under sustained 500k EPS
wal_bimodal_tail.cause: WAL writer with tick_ms=2 + 3x16MiB buffers can't keep up; apply blocks in wait_for_free_and_activate
wal_bimodal_tail.fix: Bump to 4-8 buffers x 32-64 MiB and/or longer tick (20-100ms)

allocation_hotspot.issue: WindowedOp's [Option<Box<AggOp>>; 64] preallocated for every new entity
allocation_hotspot.fix: Replace with SmallVec<[(i64, Box<AggOp>); 4]> or Vec; lazy-allocate buckets

19_0_1_wall_clock_and_wal.scope: 1-line elapsed-move + tokio::select! in get_task/rss_task + WAL config bump (4x32MiB tick=20ms)
19_0_1_wall_clock_and_wal.expected_gain: Honest +30% reported EPS for cache-hot configs; clears Phase 19 M4 threshold

19_0_2_lazy_buckets.scope: Replace WindowedOp's [Option<Box<AggOp>>; 64] with SmallVec; lazy bucket allocation

19_0_3_batch_or_op_push_many.scope: Either same-key batch sketch updates in apply dispatch, or OP_PUSH_MANY adoption in bench
19_0_3_batch_or_op_push_many.expected_gain: +30-50% EPS for zipfian/hot-key complex OR push wire-stack ceiling above 1M EPS

next_action_recommended: Path A: Open Phase 19.0.1 plan for wall_clock measurement fix + WAL config bump. Test by re-running canonical cell at N=100k zipfian. Update 19-VERIFICATION.md verdict from PASS-WITH-DEFICIT to PASS.
next_action_alternatives: Path B: Validate fraud-team.json (audit op-param schemas in agg_op.rs) and run zipfian benchmark on it
Path C: Just commit research artifacts (fraud-feature-catalogue.md + fraud-team.json with TODO comment)

advisory_constraints.constraint: Don't trust bench wall_clock_ms for N<500k until 19.0.1 lands; use send_drain_ms for bench-side rate
Cache-hot fixed shape misleads on apply work; use zipfian K=1M for realistic per-pipeline ceiling
BEAVA_TRACE_APPLY_TIMING=1 slows bench ~10x via stderr eprintln
19-VERIFICATION.md verdict needs amending after wall_clock fix

context_notes: Throughput optimization debug from Phase 19's PASS-WITH-DEFICIT verdict. User pushed back on initial 'wire-stack-bound everywhere' framing; correctly identified that fixed-shape was masking apply work. Per-op trace + complexity sweep confirmed two-regime model (wire-bound simple, apply-bound complex). Most concrete win: WindowedOp lazy buckets. Most urgent fix: wall_clock_ms measurement bug. Pause invoked at user request before applying any of the three candidate hotfixes.
